# Ziptrrip Tech questions

# Challenge 1: Answer the following five questions

## Question 1\.

**Given that you are using JavaScript.**  
**Write a function that takes n as input.**  
**And prints the following pattern.**

**1**  
**21**  
**321**  
**4321**  
**...**  
**nnnnn(n times)**

*Note:*  
Make sure the answer runs. Also, write this in different ways to achieve the same output.

**SOLUTION:**

function printPattern1(n) {  
  console.log("Way 1: Classic for loop");  
  for (let i \= 1; i \<= n; i\++) {  
    let row \= "";  
    for (let j \= i; j \>= 1; j\--) {  
      row \+= j;  
    }  
    console.log(row);  
  }  
}  
 

function printPattern2(n) {  
  console.log("Way 2: Array.from \+ join");  
  for (let i \= 1; i \<= n; i\++) {  
    const row \= Array.from({ length: i }, (\_, k) \=\> i \- k).join("");  
    console.log(row);  
  }  
}

function printPattern3(n, current \= 1) {  
  if (current \=== 1) console.log("--- Way 3: Recursive \---");  
  if (current \> n) return;  
  let row \= "";  
  for (let j \= current; j \>= 1; j\--) row \+= j;  
  console.log(row);  
  printPattern3(n, current \+ 1);  
}  
   
printPattern1(5);  
console.log();  
printPattern2(5);  
console.log();  
printPattern3(5);

**OUTPUT:**  
![][image1]

## Question 2\.

**Given that you are using JavaScript.**  
**Write some code to reverse characters in a string**  
**input:**  
**"Bhaskara"**

**output:**  
**"araksahB"**

Note:  
Make sure the answer runs. Also, write this in different ways to achieve the same output.

**SOLUTION:**  
const input \= "Bhaskara";  
   
// WAY 1: split / reverse / join  
function reverseString1(str) {  
  return str.split("").reverse().join("");  
}  
   
// WAY 2: for loop  
function reverseString2(str) {  
  let result \= "";  
  for (let i \= str.length \- 1; i \>= 0; i\--) {  
    result \+= str\[i\];  
  }  
  return result;  
}  
   
// WAY 3: spread \+ reduce  
function reverseString3(str) {  
  return \[...str\].reduce((acc, ch) \=\> ch \+ acc, "");  
}  
   
// WAY 4: Recursive  
function reverseString4(str) {  
  if (str.length \<= 1) return str;  
  return reverseString4(str.slice(1)) \+ str\[0\];  
}

console.log("Input:", input);  
console.log();  
console.log("Way 1 (split/reverse/join):", reverseString1(input));  
console.log("Way 2 (for loop)          :", reverseString2(input));  
console.log("Way 3 (spread \+ reduce)   :", reverseString3(input));  
console.log("Way 4 (recursive)         :", reverseString4(input));

**OUTPUT:**  
![][image2]

## Question 3\.

**Given that you are using JavaScript.**  
**Write some code to remove duplicates in an array and output the unique values.**  
**Input:**  
**\[ 1, 2, 3, 6, 4, 3, 7, 4, 2, 6, 8, 2, 5, 9, 0, 1 \]**

**Output:**  
**\[ 1, 2, 3, 6, 4, 7, 8, 5, 9, 0 \]**

Note:  
Make sure the answer runs. Also, write this in different ways to achieve the same output.

**SOLUTION:**  
const arr \= \[1, 2, 3, 6, 4, 3, 7, 4, 2, 6, 8, 2, 5, 9, 0, 1\];  
   
// WAY 1: Set  
function removeDuplicates1(array) {  
  return \[...new Set(array)\];  
}  
   
// WAY 2: filter \+ indexOf  
function removeDuplicates2(array) {  
  return array.filter((value, index) \=\> array.indexOf(value) \=== index);  
}  
   
// WAY 3: reduce  
function removeDuplicates3(array) {  
  return array.reduce((acc, val) \=\> {  
    if (\!acc.includes(val)) acc.push(val);  
    return acc;  
  }, \[\]);  
}  
   
// WAY 4: for loop \+ seen object  
function removeDuplicates4(array) {  
  const seen \= {};  
  const result \= \[\];  
  for (const val of array) {  
    if (\!seen\[val\]) {  
      seen\[val\] \= true;  
      result.push(val);  
    }  
  }  
  return result;  
}  
   
console.log("Input :", arr);  
console.log();  
console.log("Way 1 (Set)             :", removeDuplicates1(arr));  
console.log("Way 2 (filter+indexOf)  :", removeDuplicates2(arr));  
console.log("Way 3 (reduce)          :", removeDuplicates3(arr));  
console.log("Way 4 (for loop \+ seen) :", removeDuplicates4(arr));

**OUTPUT:**  
**![][image3]**

## Question 4\.

**Consider the following HTML**

**01 \<div id=”container”\>**  
**02    \<div class=”box”\>\</div\>**  
**03**  
**04    \<div class=”box2”\>\</div\>**  
**05    \<div\>**  
**06        \<div class=”box”\>\</div\>**  
**07    \</div\>**  
**08 \</div\>**  
**09**  
**10 \<div class=”box”\>\</div\>**

![][image4]

For each of the following selectors, write the line numbers of the elements that will be selected

`.box` 

`div .box`

`div.box`

`[class]`

`#container .box`

`#container > .box`

Note:  
Write the following for each selector

- Elements that will be selected (tell us line numbers).  
- Explain why it will select those lines.  
- Explain why it won't select other lines.

**SOLUTION:**  
.box  
Selected lines: 2, 6, 10  
Why selected: Selects any element with class "box" anywhere in the document. Lines 2, 6, 10 all have class="box".  
Why NOT others: Line 4 has class="box2" not "box", so it is not selected. 

div .box  
 Selected lines: 2, 6  
 Why selected: Descendant combinator — selects .box inside any div. Line 2 is inside \#container div. Line 6 is inside the div on line 5\.  
 Why NOT others: Line 10 is directly inside body, not inside any div, so it is not selected.

div.box  
 Selected lines: 2, 6, 10  
 Why selected: No space means element must be both a div AND have class "box". Lines 2, 6, 10 are all div class="box".  
 Why NOT others: Line 4 has class="box2" not "box", so not selected.

\[class\]  
 Selected lines: 2, 4, 6, 10  
 Why selected: Selects any element that has a class attribute (any value). Lines 2, 4, 6, 10 all have a class attribute.  
 Why NOT others: Lines 1 and 5 are plain div tags with no class attribute, so not selected.

\#container .box  
 Selected lines: 2, 6  
 Why selected: Selects .box that is a descendant of \#container. Lines 2 and 6 are both inside \#container.  
 Why NOT others: Line 10 is outside \#container, so not selected.

\#container \> .box  
 Selected lines: 2 only  
 Why selected: Direct child combinator — .box must be an immediate child of \#container. Line 2 is a direct child.  
 Why NOT others: Line 6 is a grandchild (inside line 5 div). Line 10 is outside the container. Both not selected.

## Question 5\.

**Consider the following HTML**  
![][image5]

Following needs to be achieved using CSS:

1. All 3 boxes inside the container need to be placed horizontally.  
2. First box should occupy a fixed width of 100px, and should be left aligned  
   `<div class="left fixed box"></div>` (line no 2\)  
3. Last box hould occupy a fixed width of 100px, and should be right aligned  
   `<div class="right fixed box"></div>` (line no 2\)  
4. The container can change widths, so the middle box should expand to the remaining available width between the left box and right box.  
5. The contents in the 3 boxes should not overlap.

Note:  
Write CSS to achieve the above. Also, achieve this in more than one ways and share.

**SOLUTION:**  
\<\!DOCTYPE html\>  
\<html lang\="en"\>  
\<head\>  
  \<meta charset\="UTF-8" /\>  
  \<title\>Question 5 \- 3 Box Layout\</title\>  
  \<style\>

    body {  
      font-family: Arial, sans-serif;  
      padding: 30px;  
    }

    h2 {  
      margin-top: 30px;  
      color: \#2c3e50;  
    }

    .box {  
      height: 60px;  
      line-height: 60px;  
      text-align: center;  
      color: white;  
      font-weight: bold;  
    }

    .left  { background-color: \#2980b9; }  
    .middle { background-color: \#7f8c8d; }  
    .right { background-color: \#27ae60; }

    /\* ── WAY 1 \- Flexbox ── \*/  
    \#container-flex {  
      display: flex;  
      width: 100%;  
      margin-bottom: 10px;  
    }  
    \#container-flex .left  { width: 100px; flex-shrink: 0; }  
    \#container-flex .middle { flex: 1; }  
    \#container-flex .right { width: 100px; flex-shrink: 0; }

    /\* ── WAY 2 \- Grid ── \*/  
    \#container-grid {  
      display: grid;  
      grid-template-columns: 100px 1fr 100px;  
      width: 100%;  
      margin-bottom: 10px;  
    }

    /\* ── WAY 3 \- Float ── \*/  
    \#container-float {  
      width: 100%;  
      overflow: hidden;  
      margin-bottom: 10px;  
    }  
    \#container-float .left  { float: left;  width: 100px; }  
    \#container-float .right { float: right; width: 100px; }  
    \#container-float .middle { overflow: hidden; }

  \</style\>  
\</head\>  
\<body\>

  \<h1\>Question 5 – 3 Box Layout\</h1\>

  \<h2\>Way 1 – Flexbox\</h2\>  
  \<div id\="container-flex"\>  
    \<div class\="left  box"\>Left 100px\</div\>  
    \<div class\="middle box"\>Middle \- expands\</div\>  
    \<div class\="right  box"\>Right 100px\</div\>  
  \</div\>

  \<h2\>Way 2 – CSS Grid\</h2\>  
  \<div id\="container-grid"\>  
    \<div class\="left  box"\>Left 100px\</div\>  
    \<div class\="middle box"\>Middle \- expands\</div\>  
    \<div class\="right  box"\>Right 100px\</div\>  
  \</div\>

  \<h2\>Way 3 – Float\</h2\>  
  \<div id\="container-float"\>  
    \<div class\="left  box"\>Left 100px\</div\>  
    \<div class\="right  box"\>Right 100px\</div\>  
    \<div class\="middle box"\>Middle \- expands\</div\>  
  \</div\>

\</body\>  
\</html\>

**OUTPUT:**  
**![][image6]**

# Challenge 2: Make a todo application

Frontend:   
Implement a basic react application. Application has to be multiple page instead of SPA.  
Make one page for todos list. Add the necessary features here. (refer to online todo applications for feature ideas)  
Make second page for single todo item. This page should receive a query parameter of todo id and display the todo (plus any other information that you associate it with).

Backend:  
Implement a nodejs app with expressjs.  
Create CRUD apis for todos.  
Save the data in a file or in a database. Either is fine.

To qualify:

1. Checkin the code for the above problem in a git repository and share the repo link with us.  
2. Document the features and functionalities. Undocumented features will not be considered.   
3. Documentation has to be in `.md` files in the repository itself.

SOLUTION:
GITHUB LINK: https://github.com/Yogashri25/todo-app
